programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A teenage girl goes missing while out partying with friends, and a murder investigation is launched when she is found sexually assaulted and strangled in a local park. The team has plenty of leads, with a secret camera hidden in the victim's rented car, and a local playboy admitting to inviting her on board his boat, but the suspects are all keen to point the finger at someone else. David Caruso stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda).(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Tripp pressures a suspect into confessing to the murder of a divorcee killed on her boat, but Horatio is unconvinced of his guilt and resolves to discover the truth. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell.(n)

When another man is found dead nearby, the NCIS team begins to suspect a serial killer is on the loose. Meanwhile, Ducky introduces Gibbs to his new girlfriend. Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team must save Cabe and his protege Tim when they are captured during a secret mission in Djibouti, while Sylvester hires a lawyer to help him deal with his auditions contracts.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team's mission to recover the remains of fallen Marines in Vietnam is threatened, along with their lives, when a deadly tornado heads directly towards them.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue

programme.desc: In the summer of 1966, the Northumbrian police come under increasing media scrutiny as Sunderland prepares to host a World Cup match involving the USSR, while campaigners protest against the proposed landing of the Polaris nuclear submarine at Jarrow docks. When an academic is found dead after a CND rally, Gently and Bacchus are dispatched to Durham University to investigate his background - and find themselves in the middle of a wave of social and sexual rebellion. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Sarah Lancashire and Warren Clarke.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star.(n)

programme.desc: The family prepares for baby Cameron's christening at Glenbogle, still reeling from Meg's death and struggling to care for the infant, Golly is pleased to welcome Duncan back as a godparent - but his inner turmoil deepens when Meg's sister Rosie arrives, forcing him to make a tough choice. Meanwhile, Paul faces trouble from his tenants at the AGM. Sandy Morton and Hamish Clark star.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Karen decides to concentrate all her efforts on helping Russell battle his heroin addiction _ even at the risk of destroying her marriage _ and arrives in London to find her troubled son living in a squalid bedsit. Meanwhile, Anna and Beth look after a pensioner they believe should be transferred to sheltered accommodation _ but he's none too pleased with the proposal, and promptly escapes. Drama, with Leslie Ash, Luke de Woolfson, Philip Middlemiss and guest star Leslie Phillips.(n)

programme.desc: Boycie and Tyler are united in their distaste when Marlene demands that the family pays a visit to her mother in Bournemouth. programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective struggles to cope following the death of his wife and suffers more grief when the body of his godson is discovered in a bricked-up drain 12 years after he went missing. Lynley himself becomes a suspect when the boy's sister is found dead, forcing him to enlist the help of his lawyer and DS McCaffrey's son to prove his innocence. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Nicholas Farrell.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Strickland's Whitehall pal Stephen Fisher is targeted by an assassin, so the Ucos boss enlists the team to find whoever was responsible. He reveals that 30 years earlier, he and Fisher were involved in a covert MI5 operation to break into the house of a journalist believed to have information on an IRA arms deals. But with several of the original agents having died in suspicious circumstances, he believes someone is out to kill them all - and when a connection is made between a gangster and MI5, it seems the threat may be coming from within the security service itself. Tim McInnerny and Kerry Fox guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Gramophone guru Tim Weeks takes on the repair of an antique wooden phonograph, brought into the Repair Shop by Nicola Dean, whose father was an avid collector. This particular model was his prized possession and the centrepiece of his collection. However, it suffered badly in a collision with a group of overenthusiastic small children, and the distinctive wooden horn has become separated from the main body of the item. Plus, upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary is faced with an unusual challenge when an armchair is brought in, and Steve Fletcher takes on a battered old miner's lamp, which once saved the life of its owner Hubert John.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: Three contestants are charged with transforming a living room in three Grade II Listed former workers' cottages in the town of Saltaire, West Yorkshire. programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi puts three more head chef candidates through their paces as they compete for a vacancy at Amelies, a seafood restaurant in Porthleven, Cornwall. With one candidate already through to the final, the next three applicants must endure a gruelling interview process. Amiable Irishman Barry hopes to impress with a main course of hare and rainbow pasta, while 20-year-old Chris wonders whether his decision to put a duo of lamb on a seafood menu might prove to be his undoing. Plus, experienced chef Paul refuses to let his work speak for itself as he tries to convince owner Sam he is the right man for the job.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Bryony Crutcher, 26, admits to passing off shop-bought food as her own creations, but now she's been asked to host her best friend's hen party and needs Lorraine Pascale's help to come up with an impressive meal. The food writer puts together an inspiring canape menu for Bryony to master, including Thai larb gai, bite-sized chocolate Napoleons and delicate coconut meringues filled with ginger cream.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: The Belles of St Trinian's (1954)
programme.desc: The mischievous schoolgirls hope to get rich quick by betting on a racehorse belonging to a classmate's wealthy father. However, they first have to foil a gang of thieves planning to steal the animal and outwit an undercover policewoman sent to infiltrate the school disguised as a teacher. Comedy, starring Alastair Sim, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: The pair reach Leicester, where Len Goodman braves his first ever Indian curry and samples a selection of Greek cuisine. Chef Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to create spiced fish cakes, inspired by the day's adventures in the city.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Bristol Cathedral. Antique experts James Lewis and Philip Serrell find objects to take to auction, including a bronze statue of Buddha.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cattle farmers and their protegees bring their finest cows to compete at the Driffield Show, hoping to win the much-coveted red rosettes.(n)

programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. BBC diplomatic editor and former army officer Mark Urban examines the exploits of six members of the 5th Royal Tank Regiment during the Second World War, beginning in northern France, where in 1940 rookie driver Gerry Solomon joined veterans including Jake Wardrop and Harry Finlayson. He follows in the soldiers' tracks across the deserts of North Africa, where he looks at the battles of Sidi Rezegh, Alam el Halfa and El Alamein, which changed the course of the conflict. With archive footage, including rarely seen colour film, as well as first-hand testimony from surviving veterans and letters and diaries.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents the final round of the fish course. Bestselling author Malorie Blackman joins judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver to choose the winner, with entries inspired by authors ranging from Enid Blyton to Phillip Pullman. The cooks also have a special guest in attendance in the shape of author Giles Andreae, one of whose books has been immortalised in edible form.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The second of two programmes following hospital staff treating Covid-19 cases features patients who are on dialysis or who have recently had a transplant, and as such are among the most vulnerable to the virus. Royal Free London faces a near catastrophic breakdown of oxygen supply, while an infectious diseases consultant starts a trial of an anti-viral drug used in the treatment of Ebola.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Johnny Vegas, Sue Perkins, Mike Tindall, John Barnes and Fleur East play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Peter Sellers - A State of Comic Ecstasy
programme.desc: The comedy pioneer behind the Goon Show, Dr Strangelove and the Pink Panther series is explored in depth in this film surveying his meteoric rise to fame and troubled personal life. The film charts his formative years backstage as part of his parents' itinerant music hall revue group, his years of wartime service in India and Burma, through to his global superstardom. His two surviving children Sarah and Victoria recall the challenges of growing up alongside his mood swings, while his grandson Will explores the troubled legacy his grandfather left behind.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: A couple living in a Bungalow near Brighton are treated to two virtual reality visions for what their home could become by architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison. Silvia and Julian bought the property because they loved the garden, and thought they could make something of the house - but their renovations have not been a success. Robert proposes a way to make the planning rules that have restricted them work for the project, while Laura finds a way to bring the beloved garden inside the house. Presented by Angela Scanlon.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The last heat sees amateur designers from West Yorkshire, Northampton and Loch Lomond transforming rooms in Holly Village, a set of Grade II-listed properties in north London. They each have the task of changing a bedroom in these imposing Victorian Gothic cottages - but whose vision will prove most in tune with their client? Presented by Tom Dyckhoff, with judges Kelly Hoppen and Daniel Hopwood.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The last three candidates are challenged to cook fish in a wood-fired oven as Alex continues her mission to find a head chef for Amelies seafood restaurant in Cornwall. Portuguese fisherwoman Celia takes a risk by serving up roasted octopus, Frenchman Fabien fails to tell his sea bass from his sea bream and Robert may be cool in the kitchen but struggles to take criticism. With only one place left in the final, the pressure is on.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Sonia Harwood is a mother-of-three and foster carer who would love to create homemade family meals, but ends up relying on frozen food. Lorraine shows her how easy it can be to cook with fresh ingredients, preparing dishes including courgette fritters, a quick seafood linguine and a baked ricotta cheesecake.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Blue Murder at St Trinian's (1957)
programme.desc: The mischievous schoolgirls travel by coach to Europe, accompanied by a wanted jewel thief dressed as a woman, and an undercover police detective. Comedy, starring Lionel Jeffries, Joyce Grenfell, Terry-Thomas, George Cole and Alistair Sim.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman has his first taste of Korean barbecue food and samples Swedish cuisine, and Ainsley Harriott cooks a salmon and mango salad inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall, Suffolk, where they uncover a Victorian peddler doll complete with wicker basket, while Paul Martin explores Framlingham Castle.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.sub-title: The Scottish Game Fair - Potato Vodka & Game
programme.desc: Two dedicated farming families hope to impress potential buyers at The Scottish Game Fair with farm produced potato vodka and a selection of wild game burgers fresh from the grill.(n)

programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: The two-part documentary telling the stories of six men who served in the Royal Tank Regiment concludes, with the men taking part in the D-Day landings, the battle for Normandy and the eventual victory in Germany.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Best-selling writer Anthony Horowitz joins judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver as they choose the winner for the main course, selecting the best dish that captures the wonder and imagination of children's literature, and is worthy of a banquet for the UK's finest authors and illustrators.(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: In 1900s Vienna, young English doctor Max Liebermann - studying under the famed psychoanalyst Sigmund Freud - comes into contact with Oskar Rheinhardt, a detective inspector struggling with the strange case of murder. Max offers his assistance, hoping his skills of perception and forensics and his deep understanding of human behaviour and deviance will be of use. Crime drama, starring Matthew Beard and Juergen Maurer.(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti explore one of the world's most luxurious eco resorts on the small island of Tetiaroa in French Polynesia.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary returns to follow the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in tackling the coronavirus pandemic, following staff and patients at London's Royal Free Trust from the first day of the lockdown. The Royal Free Hospital's emergency department is inundated with patients displaying symptoms, among them 88-year-old Peter, who is struggling for breath. He is quickly put on oxygen and moved to what was the coronary care unit, but has now become a ward for covid-positive patients.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The second of two programmes following hospital staff treating Covid-19 cases features patients who are on dialysis or who have recently had a transplant, and as such are among the most vulnerable to the virus. Royal Free London faces a near catastrophic breakdown of oxygen supply, while an infectious diseases consultant starts a trial of an anti-viral drug used in the treatment of Ebola.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams embark on the final leg of their journey, heading off on the 4,800 kilometre trek to the finish line in Ushuaia, Argentina, the most southerly city in the world. However, as they prepare to leave the last checkpoint, one of the teams reveals that they have run out of money and will not be completing the challenge. The three remaining pairs rely on different tactics to get ahead, benefiting from risky gambles, lucky opportunities and the kindness of strangers.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A retired serviceman who drove a burning boat out of a Washington marina to prevent the flames spreading, and the rescue of a mountain climber who fell 20ft down a rock face.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: A look at how the coronavirus crisis has revived the national spirit, with families, friends and neighbours pulling together and communities uniting in the face of adversity.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: As the UK faces its biggest challenge since the Second World War, Sophie Raworth looks at how the nation has responded to adversity in the past and in the present. Using a combination of wartime archive and contemporary news footage, each episode looks at the impact of Covid-19 on families, communities and businesses, and compares how the nation has responded to crisis now and then.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team create a £5000 garden mixing a Victorian past with modern style and bold style and colours for opera singer Catherine in Shrewsbury.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns to the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, discovering how plant-based remedies were mixed and bottled by Victorian pharmacists. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with James Bond memorabilia making an appearance alongside a miniature steam engine and a rare religious ring.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees two designers take on a living room and a sitting room each in Mock Tudor houses in Worsley near Salford. They try to transform rooms in Mock Tudor houses with an increased budget of £2.000 each for their latest tasks. This time they'll have two builders and two decorators to manage, and hope they'll do enough to impress judges Kelly Hoppen and Daniel Hopwood.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The finalists are challenged to produce a three-course wedding menu to be taste-tested by the manager's family and friends. However, the enormity of the task takes its toll, resulting in a burnt main course and undercooked pear tart, and owner Sam has a difficult decision to make before one competitor is given the job of head chef.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Neil Forster is an impatient cook who reaches for the takeaway menu rather than making food from scratch. Lorraine shows him recipes including asparagus and cream cheese puff pastry tarts, a quick way to do a roast chicken and a neat shortcut to prepare a summer fruits pudding.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: The Pure Hell of St Trinian's (1960)
programme.desc: The girls burn their school to the ground, prompting a mysterious Middle Eastern gentleman to offer new premises for their studies - but he has a sinister ulterior motive for helping the badly behaved youngsters. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, Joyce Grenfell, George Cole and Irene Handl.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman and Ainsley Harriott reach Cardiff, where the Strictly judge has his first taste of spaghetti and samples some German hospitality, and Ainsley cooks an Italian dish.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Royal Marines Museum in Hampshire. Charles Hanson looks over a boxing glove signed Henry Cooper, and Catherine Southon learns about naval traditions.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Chris Ramsey and broadcasters Anita Rani, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary asks why people turn to music in times of crisis, from free concerts streamed by A-list names to benefit singles, cross-balcony singalongs and quarantine choirs. She also asks whether the spirit of sharing and caring change the way we consume music in the long term, or whether the generosity will run out if and when we ‘get back to normal'.(n)

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorer's trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts England's highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the country's most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining regional finalists prepare their desserts, hoping to secure the final place on the banquet for the UK's finest children's authors and illustrators. The chefs serve up dishes inspired by a wide range of books from Matilda to Paddington Bear, with radio DJ Greg James joining the judges to choose their favourite.(n)

programme.title: Charlie Brooker's Antiviral Wipe
programme.desc: The writer and broadcaster returns to front his own show since the award-winning Charlie Brooker's 2016 Wipe, to take a satirical look at life under lockdown. As well as the crisis itself, he will be finding out what people have been watching to while away the hours, with help from the ever-insightful Philomena Cunk.(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.sub-title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019: Jumbo All-in-One Edition
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk and other contributors take a look back at the major events of last year, including Brexit, climate change, Theresa May, Prince Andrew, Trump, Joker and Game of Thrones.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Desiree Burch hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Paul McCaffrey and Jonny Pelham.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Big Animal Surgery
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin travels to Tbilisi in Georgia where she watches an operation on an Asian elephant named Grande. Having survived a catastrophic flood at the zoo where he lives, Grande has a badly infected tusk which needs removing. But the vet is working against the clock as every minute Grande is under anaesthetic, the weight of his own body risks damaging his vital organs.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints birch trees, surrounded by a misty background a cheerful blue sky.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Books
programme.desc: A look at the reading material of medieval times, focusing on the shift from manuscripts in the 14th and 15th centuries to the literature that followed. The programme focuses on The Luttrell Psalter, a book of psalms that featured humorous and vivid pictures of rural life and a grotesque demonic world, and Geoffrey Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, which was adopted by William Caxton as the first publication to be created by his printing press in London.(n)

programme.title: Life Drawing Live: Drawing The Nation Together
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby hosts a live broadcast of a life drawing class, allowing viewers to draw along with the students in the socially distanced studio. The models present a variety of poses inspired by classical works of art, while artists Lachlan Goudie, Diana Ali and Nicky Philipps are on hand to share their passion and knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC: The Genius of Leonardo da Vinci
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez explores the BBC archives to explore the work of the Renaissance artist, revealing what lies beneath the Mona Lisa and how Da Vinci acquired his anatomical knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Da Vinci Code - The Greatest Story Ever Sold
programme.desc: Timeshift explores the contemporary quest for ancient truths that has arisen out of Dan Brown's best-selling novel challenging the credibility of Christian doctrine. The programme assesses the explosive response to the book from believers and atheists. With contributions by author Richard Leigh and art critic Brian Sewell.(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines the surprising climax of the Renaissance, a period full of war, confusion and darkness, which was captured perfectly in the art of Leonardo, Bosch, Arcimboldo, Palissy, the Italian mannerists and El Greco.(n)

programme.title: Jonathan Meades on Jargon
programme.desc: The writer and broadcaster dissects politics, the law, football commentary, business, the arts, tabloid-speak and management consultancy to show how jargon is used to cover up, confuse and keep the public in the dark.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints birch trees, surrounded by a misty background a cheerful blue sky.(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC: The Genius of Leonardo da Vinci
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez explores the BBC archives to explore the work of the Renaissance artist, revealing what lies beneath the Mona Lisa and how Da Vinci acquired his anatomical knowledge.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a cozy barn in winter, with the ground blanketed in snow, set against a soft sky.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Books
programme.desc: How illustrated books became an established genre owing to the Victorians' new printing technology, an emerging middle-class readership and a sentimentalised regard for childhood and fairy tales. The programme reveals how the writer and illustrator partnership has continued to enrich books, from Lewis Carroll and John Tenniel's Alice in Wonderland to Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's The Gruffalo.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.desc: The historian examines Kibworth's school archive and learns how peasant education was organised in the 14th century, with people establishing their own educational centres. Evidence also reveals that some villagers rebelled against Henry V and others aspired to become merchants in the textile town of Coventry.(n)

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Photo Album
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley tells the story of the Royal photograph, showing how the Royal family worked with generations of photographers to create images that reinvented the British monarchy.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Photographed
programme.sub-title: The World's Most Photographed: Queen Victoria
programme.desc: An exploration of how the camera transformed the relationship between the monarchy and the British people during the 64-year reign of Queen Victoria, whose accession to the throne in 1837 coincided with the invention of photography. The monarch, whose private life became more visible than ever before, managed to turn the pursuit from a pastime into a political weapon in her battle to safeguard the British throne.(n)

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian reveals a story of invasion in Britain spanning millennia, beginning with him felling a tree with a flint axe in Kent, and riding an Iron Age chariot. Sam Willis also searches for clues of invasion at Silbury Hill, and tracks down evidence of the Beaker people, who brought ceramics, metalwork and beakers to Britain.(n)

programme.title: The Quest for Bannockburn
programme.desc: Part one of two. To mark the 700th anniversary of Robert the Bruce's decisive victory in the First War of Scottish Independence at Bannockburn, Neil Oliver and Tony Pollard attempt to pinpoint the location of Scotland's most iconic battlefield. They are joined by historians, archaeologists and environmental scientists as they set out to gather evidence to chart the events that ended in an overwhelming Scottish victory over Edward II's English forces, helping seal Scotland's future as an independent kingdom.(n)

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores how the art and culture produced by cities past and present has shaped the country's attitudes and helped forge the very idea of Japan itself.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a cozy barn in winter, with the ground blanketed in snow, set against a soft sky.(n)

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How Australian cinema celebrates the endurance of outsiders, whether they are newcomers to a strange new land in They're a Weird Mob and Wake in Fright, or locals out of step with the mainstream in Evil Angels, Muriel's Wedding and Priscilla, Queen of the Desert.(n)

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Photo Album
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley tells the story of the Royal photograph, showing how the Royal family worked with generations of photographers to create images that reinvented the British monarchy.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a seascape, with crashing waves and a sky made of a mixture of gold, pink and mauve.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Books
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of paperback cover design in the 20th century and how it became a way of selling a book in the mass market. The programme looks at the changing designs on novels such as George Orwell's Nineteen Eighty-Four, drawing in new readers by reflecting each decade, as well as the advent of electronic readers and how novels face more scrutiny as they compete with technology.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel.(n)

programme.title: The BBC Lockdown Orchestra: You Got the Love
programme.desc: A virtual performance of the Candi Staton disco classic by musicians from all six BBC orchestras, choirs and the Ulster Orchestra - augmented by people joining in from home.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the first episode, satellites follow an elephant family which is struggling through a drought, reveal previously unknown penguin colonies, and discover mysterious ice rings that could put seal pups in danger.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.sub-title: A Place Beyond the Sky - Part One
programme.desc: Documentary detailing America's determination to put a man on the Moon ahead of Soviet Russia, beginning with how the launch of Sputnik 1 in 1957 compelled the USA to join the space race.(n)

programme.title: Science and Islam
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili continues his investigation into scientific methods originating from the Islamic world with a trip to Northern Syria, where he learns about Al-Biruni, the astronomer and mathematician who, 1,000 years ago, estimated the size of Earth to within a few hundred miles of the correct figure. He also visits Cairo to explore physicist Ibn al-Haytham's contribution to the modern science of optics.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme celebrates its 800th episode, with filming taking place under lockdown, as Chris Lintott explores how some astronomers are managing to continue making observations - including controlling telescopes as far away as Hawaii from a laptop in their bedrooms in Belfast.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group begin their second week, having settled into their new home on the coast of Puducherry. Henry Blofeld is keen to find a cure for an old injury, so he and Paul Chuckle visit a local hospital where western medicine is combined with yoga. Paul also braves the chaotic roads as he throws himself headlong into his first Indian driving lesson, while Britt Ekland and Zandra Rhodes explore the more spiritual side of their temporary home. Later in the week, some of the group catch the sleeper train to Madurai, where they enjoy the wild celebrations of the famous Chithirai festival.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team return to Veterans Street in Manchester, where, in 2015, with the help of Prince William and Prince Harry, DIY SOS launched their most ambitious project yet - transforming a derelict street into a vibrant community for veterans and local people. This time they build the final home for decorated former soldier and single dad Simon Flores, whose foot was blown off by a roadside bomb during a patrol in Iraq.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group begin their second week, having settled into their new home on the coast of Puducherry. Henry Blofeld is keen to find a cure for an old injury, so he and Paul Chuckle visit a local hospital where western medicine is combined with yoga. Paul also braves the chaotic roads as he throws himself headlong into his first Indian driving lesson, while Britt Ekland and Zandra Rhodes explore the more spiritual side of their temporary home. Later in the week, some of the group catch the sleeper train to Madurai, where they enjoy the wild celebrations of the famous Chithirai festival.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Lockdown: How Business Is Coping - Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's economy has been turned upside down by the coronavirus crisis. Many companies are struggling to stay afloat, while some have found themselves swamped with unprecedented demand. Panorama follows some of the small business owners and key workers struggling through the lockdown - from the courier whose job it is to collect suspected samples of Covid-19, to the funeral director working round the clock to collect and bury the dead.(n)

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)

programme.desc: The work of brothers Allan and Ewen Maclean at their bakery in Benbecula, which they opened more than 30 years ago, beginning with an introduction to the team.(n)

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie Macleod heads to Barra, where he meets Chrissie whose porch was built 30 years ago. The weather and the years have left their mark on it, but Donnie has a solution that leaves Chrissie with a warm, dry porch in which to sit and complete her crosswords.(n)

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: A set by Welsh singer-songwriter Gruff Rhys, recorded in August 2016, featuring acoustic versions of Cryndod Yn Dy Lais, Ymaelodi A'r Ymylon and Nythod Cacwn.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis presents a new music series for our times, offering musicians an opportunity to perform directly from their homes to viewing fans.(n)

programme.title: Ameireaga air Film (America in Colour)
programme.desc: Life in 1960s America, shown in colour for the first time. The decade began with a crucial election between Richard Nixon and John F Kennedy and ended on a high as Apollo 11 launches - Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin become the first men to walk on the moon.(n)

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Sian perform a song and set of puirt-à-beul at Na Trads 2019.(n)

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.sub-title: Bàs an Eich/Death of the Horse
programme.desc: Short comedy interpretation of Death of the Horse, sung by John 'Hoddan' MacDonald and starring Greg McHugh.(n)

programme.title: FUNC
programme.desc: Ragnar shows off his new boat and Flo and Marge take gin to the gym.(n)

programme.title: The Village
programme.desc: As summer begins, Delyth starts planning for the wedding season and the staff at Portmeirion celebrate the work of Susan Williams-Ellis - the pioneer behind its pottery. The programme also meets Rob Piercy, who has spent 25 years capturing the village on canvas.(n)

programme.title: How to Keep Your Dog Happy at Home
programme.desc: Tips on how to keep dogs mentally stimulated during lockdown. James Martin reveals how he looks after two pets with very different types of intelligence, and learns new tricks for keeping them both happy. Among the family pets featured are a border collie that has been trained to herd turkeys and a dog that takes great pleasure in getting stuck under a side table several times a day.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on a journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the second episode, Ryan, Adam and Scott attempt to find their inner zen at a huge meditation hall, and Scott gets a fiery fix-up Indian style by a hairdresser who prefers flames to scissors. Later, a special family meeting leads to showman Dougie hitting the stage in Mumbai.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Saving Money: Tips for Lockdown - Tonight
programme.desc: With pay cuts, job losses and businesses shut down for weeks, the financial impact of coronavirus is hitting people hard. Helen Skelton investigates the best ways for people to tighten their belts and try to save money.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show Live
programme.desc: Everyday life has changed dramatically with everyone facing more restrictions - and more uncertainty. Martin Lewis presents another live special in which he and Angellica Bell will be examining how coronavirus could affect finances, and taking viewers' questions on specific issues.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Thea already has a car sitting at home and is desperate to pass - if she stops talking long enough to listen to her examiner. Also, Mollie and Cheryl are back, hoping to improve on their catastrophic last tests. For Mollie, it would be the end of 16 years of trying - and failing - to get on the road.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp talks about his life and career as one of the most respected and experienced football managers in the modern game, as well as his victorious I'm a Celebrity appearance in 2018, when he was crowned King of the Jungle. He discusses the many twists and turns of his rollercoaster life, including a road accident in 1990 in which his friend Brian Tiler died. Harry also opens up about a dramatic dawn raid on his house and the death threats he received when he became manager of Southampton.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village newcomer Vivienne Mackay is found battered to death in her own kitchen after announcing her intention to marry businessman Dominic Bellingham - who has not yet separated from his current wife. Dawn seizes an opportunity to make a fast buck using Vince's hot dog van on the day of a big football match but finds herself in cut-throat competition with Peggy and her baked potato operation. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice.(n)

programme.desc: Kim is amazed when Frank tells her he has decided to let her and Dave run the business. Chris confronts Kim about her affair. The Dingle family worry about Zak.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village newcomer Vivienne Mackay is found battered to death in her own kitchen after announcing her intention to marry businessman Dominic Bellingham - who has not yet separated from his current wife. PC Mason is reminded of his own mother's death, and personal feelings threaten to cloud his professional judgement. Dawn seizes an opportunity to make a fast buck using Vince's hot dog van on the day of a big football match but finds herself in cut-throat competition with Peggy and her baked potato operation. Nikki Sanderson and Gwen Taylor star.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: The Happiest Days of Your Life - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The twins abscond from Brockhurst College, but Pop manages to bring them back safe and sound - then lends his services to ensure the boarding school does not have to close. Meanwhile, Primrose makes an unusual request concerning affairs of the heart and Charley launches a bid to buy a local brewery. David Jason stars.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is still trying to come to terms with Nellie's departure. Chris is determined not to let Kim win. Tina asks Terry if there is any work available at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A mystery figure takes photos of Kim and Dave together. Frank arrives home from hospital. Biff and Linda get engaged but decide not to tell anyone.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek is determined to catch whoever is harassing him. Kevin is uncomfortable with Bill spending money on the family. Deirdre calls the police out when she hears a prowler.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre puts Jamie up when Tricia doesn't return home. The Duckworths can't sleep for worrying about money. Rita tells Bill he might be getting in the way at the Websters.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Egyptian Tomb
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Bancroft is faced with a blast from the past that brings events from 1990 to the surface, and Katherine wrestles with new information that she is uncovering. Elsewhere, Daanish fulfils his part of the deal, and Anya makes a surprising visits to an old friend. Starring Sarah Parish, Faye Marsay and Amara Karan.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Cast Not Your Pearls Before Swine - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop plans to build a swimming pool in the shape of Ma for her birthday surprise, while Charley and Mariette take a well-earned break in France. Starring David Jason and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pete and Frank set up a hidden camera at Home Farm to monitor Kim. Chris has a business proposal for Jack and Sarah. The dance competition gets under way.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Jan confront Dave about the affair. Seth gives Biff some marriage advice. Vic and Viv discover Scott's gun and attempt to wrestle it off him, but the trigger goes off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths plan how to spend their money. Liz thinks she's pregnant. Curly worries when Reg and Eric have exclusive meetings without him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The McDonalds struggle with their feelings about Liz's pregnancy. Jack and Vera make plans to buy the shop. The Websters agree that Sally should tell Bill he has to move out.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Katherine and Anya make a determined push to get to the bottom of the Laura Fraser case once and for all, hatching a plan to get the DNA evidence they desperately need. Bancroft executes a massive sting operation against Athif Kamara, but things do not run smoothly and she is forced to reveal carefully kept secrets about her past. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, Faye Marsay and Amara Khan.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise makes plans to redecorate. Alec offers Steve £5,000 to leave Vicky alone. Derek worries that everyone is laughing at him behind his back.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill is nervous about meeting Sally. Audrey tells Alma the gossip about Mike and Deirdre having an affair. Vicky has some harsh words for Alec.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles prepare to help Albert give himself up. Kim warns Nick not to cross her again. Frank bides his time before letting Kim know he knows about the affair.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rick and Biff's fight spills out on to the street. Frank tells Zoe that the baby isn't his and that Kim has been having an affair. Eric demands his money back from the Dingles.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flood of forged banknotes leads the police to believe a fugitive gangster has arrived in Aidensfield, but their efforts to flush him out put Dawson in peril. Meanwhile, Miller tries to remain professional while dealing with his relationship problems and Bernie thinks his favourite celebrity has moved in down the road. Gemma Craven guest stars, with Clare Wille, John Duttine, Peter Benson and Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: The Happiest Days of Your Life - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Primrose falls for a roofing contractor, while the twins prove too much of a handful for their school, prompting a trip to view the facilities on offer at Brockhurst College. Nostalgic comedy drama, starring David Jason and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin gets the chance to play for his college football team - but a cold threatens to earn him an early bath. Flat-share comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village newcomer Vivienne Mackay is found battered to death in her own kitchen after announcing her intention to marry businessman Dominic Bellingham - who has not yet separated from his current wife. PC Mason is reminded of his own mother's death, and personal feelings threaten to cloud his professional judgement. Dawn seizes an opportunity to make a fast buck using Vince's hot dog van on the day of a big football match but finds herself in cut-throat competition with Peggy and her baked potato operation. Nikki Sanderson and Gwen Taylor star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe reluctantly agrees to help Frank with his revenge plan. Rachel tell Chris all about Kim's affair. A drunken Zak causes a scene in the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is amazed when Frank tells her he has decided to let her and Dave run the business. Chris confronts Kim about her affair. The Dingle family worry about Zak.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill feels terrible about the gutted kitchen. Alec refuses to say goodbye as Steve and Vicky leave for St Lucia. Deirdre tells Alma that the rumours about her and Mike aren't true.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin isn't happy about having Bill sleep on the sofa. The Duckworths return from Spain to find the inheritance money hasn't arrived. A mysterious parcel arrives at the Wiltons.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side

Nikki Sanderson and Gwen Taylor star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Katherine and Joe's relationship progresses, while events take a grisly turn for the Kamara family - forcing Bancroft to offer Daanish a deal. Laura's former husband Tim Fraser is reluctant to help with Laura's cold case, but an unexpected visit changes his mind, and Katherine and Anya make some game-changing discoveries.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett.(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop plans to build a swimming pool in the shape of Ma for her birthday surprise, while Charley and Mariette take a well-earned break in France. Starring David Jason and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pete and Frank set up a hidden camera at Home Farm to monitor Kim. Chris has a business proposal for Jack and Sarah. The dance competition gets under way.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Jan confront Dave about the affair. Seth gives Biff some marriage advice. Vic and Viv discover Scott's gun and attempt to wrestle it off him, but the trigger goes off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths plan how to spend their money. Liz thinks she's pregnant. Curly worries when Reg and Eric have exclusive meetings without him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The McDonalds struggle with their feelings about Liz's pregnancy. Jack and Vera make plans to buy the shop. The Websters agree that Sally should tell Bill he has to move out.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Katherine and Anya make a determined push to get to the bottom of the Laura Fraser case once and for all, hatching a plan to get the DNA evidence they desperately need. Bancroft executes a massive sting operation against Athif Kamara, but things do not run smoothly and she is forced to reveal carefully kept secrets about her past. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, Faye Marsay and Amara Khan.(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The duo try to track down an American colonel tormented by memories his harrowing experiences while in command of an air base in Vietnam. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner investigates the death of a college student who drowned during a hazing ritual, leaving his family struggling to cope with their grief.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A desperate boxer falsely accused of murdering his wife takes a priest and two of his parishioners hostage. Theo must stop him hurting anyone while also uncovering the real killer.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An interpreter claims to have been abducted by two villains who forced him to interrogate a badly bruised and obviously distressed Greek captive unable to speak English. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A tape implicating two senior police officers in a criminal conspiracy is mistakenly delivered to Daley Videos, and Arthur and Terry find themselves placed in contact with its owner - a local major crime boss. Ian McShane, Rula Lenska, Brian Blessed and Ray Winstone guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates the fatal shooting of a South American president's son at a polo match, and finds that the victim's step-sister is unknowingly dating one of the terrorists involved. Ruby Wax and Pierce Brosnan feature in early screen appearances, alongside stars Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan adopts a new identity as an ex-con and goes undercover as a truck driver to expose a gang of ruthless hijackers. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A girl injured in a road accident dies mysteriously in hospital, and Quincy's investigation leads him to some disquieting numbers about emergency room care.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.sub-title: When You Hear the Beep, Drop Dead
programme.desc: Several witnesses overhear a prostitute plotting to murder her lover, but when Theo investigates, he cannot find any trace of the woman in question, or her victim's body.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A solicitor enlists Holmes to help clear him of murdering eccentric builder Jonas Oldacre, whose considerable personal fortune he stood to inherit. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the famed Victorian detective.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur panics when he learns that there has been a robbery of safety deposit boxes in the City, so places all his savings in the ancient safe at the lock-up. Unfortunately, they soon catch the eye of an old acquaintance from Marbella. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's schoolteacher girlfriend seeks CI5's assistance to quash a drug-smuggling ring after learning a 14-year-old pupil is involved having used then hidden a discarded firearm. The body count soon increases at an alarming rate as potential witnesses to a crime are killed off by the ruthless gang. Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Celia Gregory star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan becomes romantically involved with a crime journalist. However, when she is implicated in a major crime, her innocence and his professional loyalties are called into question. Carter also wavers, beginning to fear his boss's personal life has shadowed his judgement. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove buy and transform a BSA Bantam from a humble roadster into a trial bike. However, they soon discover it is far easier to restore than it is to sell.(n)

programme.title: Get Carter (1971)
programme.desc: Racketeer Jack Carter returns to his native Newcastle to attend his brother's funeral and investigate the circumstances surrounding his death. As events unfold, he uncovers troubling connections to drugs, pornography and corruption, and finds himself on a collision-course with a criminal kingpin and his cohorts. Mike Hodges' thriller, starring Michael Caine, Ian Hendry, Britt Ekland, Glynn Edwards and John Osborne.(n)

Mike Hodges' thriller, starring Michael Caine, Ian Hendry, Britt Ekland, Glynn Edwards and John Osborne.(n)

programme.title: The Missouri Breaks (1976)
programme.desc: An eccentric harpoon-wielding bounty hunter is hired by a rancher to dispose of a gang of rustlers bent on revenge for the hanging of one of their henchmen. However, he becomes obsessed with finishing the job no matter what gets in his way - even after his employer fires him. Western, starring Jack Nicholson, Marlon Brando, Harry Dean Stanton, Randy Quaid, Kathleen Lloyd and Frederic Forrest.(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: This second series of the action drama finds Roger Murtaugh attempting to steer clear of the demons that haunt his partner Martin Riggs, while navigating some challenging times at home. In this instalment, the mismatched duo respond to the death of a plastic surgeon, drawing them into a case involving an illegal drug ring run out of a wellness clinic. Things heat up when Palmer from the DEA and Riggs compete to solve the case first. Meanwhile, Trish and Murtaugh face the emotional challenge of sending their first son, RJ, off to college. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star.(n)

programme.sub-title: I Was Happy Where I Was

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A doctor visits Holmes on behalf of his friend and sponsor Blessington, a nervous invalid whose fragile peace of mind has been thrown into turmoil by a series of mysterious events. Drama, guest starring Patrick Newell and Nicholas Clay, with Jeremy Brett and David Burke.(n)

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A crooked foreign diplomat helps arrange the theft of a consignment of silver bullion being transported through Tilbury docks. Bodie and Doyle are assigned to investigate, and it becomes apparent that they have a useful link to the villains via Doyle's girlfriend Deborah. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter uncover a plot to smuggle gold bars into the Middle East disguised as weights for an international strongman competition. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)
